title: acaena longiscapa
intro: Acaena longiscapa, also known as the long- winged variegated caterpillar-bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to New Zealand. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional Maori medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Acaena longiscapa is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. The herbal preparation of Acaena longiscapa typically involves drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant in a topical ointment or salve to apply directly to the skin.
